C4: `validation` split of `znhoughton/c4-subset-10B-tokens`).
This removes the optimistic bias of training perplexity and gives a true picture
of model quality at each checkpoint.

```{r delta-ll-val-perplexity}
VAL_PPL_CSV <- normalizePath(
  file.path(dirname(DATA_DIR), "training_quality", "val_perplexity_curves.csv"),
  mustWork = FALSE
)

if (!file.exists(VAL_PPL_CSV)) {
  message("val_perplexity_curves.csv not found — run compute_checkpoint_val_perplexity.py first.")
} else {
  val_ppl <- read_csv(VAL_PPL_CSV, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
    rename(model_size = params) |>
    mutate(model_size = factor(model_size, levels = SIZE_LEVELS))

  # Join validation perplexity to delta_ll_traj via nearest step
  delta_ll_val <- delta_ll_traj |>
    select(-perplexity) |>        # drop training perplexity
    left_join(
      val_ppl |> rename(lc_step = step),
      by = c("corpus", "model_size"),
      relationship = "many-to-many"
    ) |>
    mutate(step_dist = abs(step - lc_step)) |>
    group_by(corpus, model_size, step) |>
    slice_min(step_dist, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
    ungroup() |>
    select(-lc_step, -step_dist) |>
    filter(!is.na(val_perplexity))

  # ── Slope significance tests (validation perplexity) ─────────────────────
  slope_tests_val <- delta_ll_val |>
    group_by(corpus, model_size) |>
    group_modify(function(d, k) {
      if (nrow(d) < 3) return(tibble(slope = NA_real_, se = NA_real_, t = NA_real_, p = NA_real_, n = nrow(d)))
      fit <- lm(delta_ll ~ log2(val_perplexity), data = d)
      cf  <- summary(fit)$coefficients
      tibble(
        slope = cf["log2(val_perplexity)", "Estimate"],
        se    = cf["log2(val_perplexity)", "Std. Error"],
        t     = cf["log2(val_perplexity)", "t value"],
        p     = cf["log2(val_perplexity)", "Pr(>|t|)"],
        n     = nrow(d)
      )
    }) |>
    ungroup() |>
    mutate(
      sig = case_when(p < .001 ~ "***", p < .01 ~ "**", p < .05 ~ "*", p < .10 ~ ".", TRUE ~ "ns"),
      label = sprintf("β = %.3f, t(%d) = %.2f, p %s %s",
                      slope, n - 2L, t,
                      ifelse(p < .001, "<", "="),
                      ifelse(p < .001, ".001", sprintf("%.3f", p)),
                      sig)
    )

  cat("\nSlope tests (validation perplexity): log2(val_perplexity) → ΔLL\n")
  print(slope_tests_val |> select(corpus, model_size, slope, se, t, p, sig, n), n = Inf)

  label_pts_val <- delta_ll_val |>
    group_by(corpus, model_size) |>
    slice_min(val_perplexity, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
    ungroup()

  print(
    ggplot(delta_ll_val,
           aes(x = val_perplexity, y = delta_ll,
               colour = model_size, shape = model_size)) +
      facet_wrap(~ corpus, scales = "free_x") +
      geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.45) +
      geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = TRUE,
                  linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.12) +
      geom_text(data = label_pts_val,
                aes(label = model_size),
                hjust = -0.15, vjust = -0.4,
                size  = 3.8, fontface = "bold",
                show.legend = FALSE) +
      scale_x_continuous(
        "Validation perplexity (lower \u2192 better trained)",
        trans  = "log2",
        labels = scales::trans_format("log2", scales::math_format(2^.x))
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous("\u0394LL") +
      scale_colour_manual(values = size_colours, name = "Model size") +
      scale_shape_manual(values  = size_shapes,  name = "Model size") +
      guides(colour = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, alpha = 1)),
             shape  = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, alpha = 1))) +
      labs(
        title    = "\u0394LL vs. validation perplexity across training checkpoints",
        subtitle = paste0(
          "\u0394LL = logLik(resp_alpha \u223c freq_prob_c + model pref) \u2212 logLik(resp_alpha \u223c freq_prob_c). ",
          "Each point = one checkpoint. Dashed lines = loess smoother."
        )
      ) +
      theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
      theme(
        legend.position   = "none",
        strip.background  = element_blank(),
        strip.text        = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
        panel.grid.major  = element_line(colour = "grey92", linewidth = 0.4),
        axis.line         = element_line(colour = "grey40"),
        axis.ticks        = element_line(colour = "grey40")
      )
  )
}
```

## Model vs. Human α-Ordering Proportions

For each binomial, we compare two proportions:

- **Human proportion α**: fraction of human participants who chose the α ordering (e.g., "salt and pepper" over "pepper and salt"), computed across all individual trials in `all_human_data.csv`.
- **Model proportion α**: out of the 51 sentence-frame prompts used to score each binomial, the fraction where the model's log-odds preference was positive (i.e., `preference > 0`), computed from the raw per-prompt checkpoint CSVs, **separately per corpus × model size**.

Each point is one binomial. Plot 1 uses the final training checkpoint; Plot 2 shows how the model–human correlation (Pearson *r*) evolves across training.

```{r model-vs-human-alpha-prop}
CKPT_DIR <- normalizePath(
  file.path(dirname(DATA_DIR), "checkpoint_results"),
  mustWork = FALSE
)
N_PROMPTS <- 51L   # number of sentence-frame prompts in model-prefs-all-ckpts.py

# Helper: load per-prompt preferences for one (model, step) checkpoint CSV
load_ckpt_raw <- function(model_name, step) {
  short <- sub("znhoughton/", "", model_name)
  path  <- file.path(CKPT_DIR, sprintf("%s_step-%d.csv", short, step))
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    warning("Checkpoint CSV not found: ", path)
    return(NULL)
  }
  read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE, col_select = c(binom, preference))
}

# Human proportion α per binomial (pooled across all participants)
human_alpha_prop <- human_trials |>
  group_by(binom) |>
  summarise(prop_human_alpha = mean(resp_alpha), n_humans = n(), .groups = "drop")

# ── Plot 1: final checkpoint ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
message("Loading final-checkpoint CSVs for model-vs-human plot ...")
raw_final <- final_ck_meta |>
  mutate(
    corpus     = parse_corpus(model),
    model_size = factor(parse_size(model), levels = SIZE_LEVELS)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(corpus), !is.na(model_size)) |>
  rowwise() |>
  reframe({
    d <- load_ckpt_raw(model, step)
    if (is.null(d)) tibble() else
      mutate(d, corpus = corpus, model_size = model_size, step = step)
  })

prop_final <- raw_final |>
  filter(!is.na(preference)) |>
  group_by(corpus, model_size, binom) |>
  summarise(
    prop_model_alpha = mean(preference > 0),
    n_prompts        = n(),
    .groups          = "drop"
  ) |>
  inner_join(human_alpha_prop, by = "binom")

cors_final <- prop_final |>
  group_by(corpus, model_size) |>
  summarise(r = cor(prop_human_alpha, prop_model_alpha),
            lbl = sprintf("r = %.2f", r), .groups = "drop")

prop_plot_theme <- list(
  facet_grid(model_size ~ corpus),
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", colour = "grey50"),
  geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.40, colour = "#2166ac"),
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", formula = y ~ x, se = TRUE,
              linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.9,
              colour = "#b2182b", fill = "#b2182b", alpha = 0.12),
  scale_x_continuous("Proportion of humans choosing \u03b1 ordering",
                     limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)),
  scale_y_continuous(sprintf("Proportion of %d prompts where model prefers \u03b1", N_PROMPTS),
                     limits = c(0, 1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25)),
  theme_classic(base_size = 12),
  theme(strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
        panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey92", linewidth = 0.4),
        axis.line        = element_line(colour = "grey40"),
        axis.ticks       = element_line(colour = "grey40"))
)

ggplot(prop_final, aes(x = prop_human_alpha, y = prop_model_alpha)) +
  prop_plot_theme +
  geom_text(data = cors_final, aes(label = lbl),
            x = 0.05, y = 0.97, hjust = 0, vjust = 1,
            size = 3.2, fontface = "italic", inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title    = "Model vs. human \u03b1-ordering proportions \u2014 final checkpoint",
    subtitle = "Each point = one binomial. Dotted = perfect agreement. Dashed = loess fit."
  )

# ── Plot 2: trajectory of model–human correlation over training ───────────────
message("Loading sampled-checkpoint CSVs for trajectory plot (may take a moment) ...")
raw_traj <- ck_meta |>
  mutate(
    corpus     = parse_corpus(model),
    model_size = factor(parse_size(model), levels = SIZE_LEVELS)
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(corpus), !is.na(model_size)) |>
  rowwise() |>
  reframe({
    d <- load_ckpt_raw(model, step)
    if (is.null(d)) tibble() else
      mutate(d, corpus = corpus, model_size = model_size, step = step)
  })

r_traj <- raw_traj |>
  filter(!is.na(preference)) |>
  group_by(corpus, model_size, step, binom) |>
  summarise(prop_model_alpha = mean(preference > 0), .groups = "drop") |>
  inner_join(human_alpha_prop, by = "binom") |>
  group_by(corpus, model_size, step) |>
  summarise(r = cor(prop_human_alpha, prop_model_alpha), .groups = "drop") |>
  left_join(
    loss_curves |> rename(lc_step = step),
    by = c("corpus", "model_size"),
    relationship = "many-to-many"
  ) |>
  mutate(step_dist = abs(step - lc_step)) |>
  group_by(corpus, model_size, step) |>
  slice_min(step_dist, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup() |>
  select(-lc_step, -step_dist)

ggplot(r_traj,
       aes(x = perplexity, y = r,
           colour = model_size, shape = model_size)) +
  facet_wrap(~ corpus, scales = "free_x") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    "Training perplexity (lower \u2192 better trained)",
    trans  = "log2",
    labels = scales::trans_format("log2", scales::math_format(2^.x))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous("Pearson r (model prop \u03b1 vs human prop \u03b1)") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = size_colours, name = "Model size") +
  scale_shape_manual(values  = size_shapes,  name = "Model size") +
  labs(
    title    = "Model\u2013human \u03b1-ordering agreement over training",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "r = Pearson correlation between model prop \u03b1 (%d prompts) and human prop \u03b1, per checkpoint.",
      N_PROMPTS
    )
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey92", linewidth = 0.4),
    axis.line        = element_line(colour = "grey40"),
    axis.ticks       = element_line(colour = "grey40"),
    legend.position  = "right"
  )
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Section 3: Case Studies — Full Training Trajectories

Take the top **`r N_CASE_BINOMS`** 125M-disagreement items per corpus (largest
`|signed_gap|` in 125M, AbsPref predicts correct direction) and plot their
full training trajectories for all three model sizes.

All values are on the **P(α) − 0.5** scale (0 = no preference; +0.5 = always α; −0.5 = always β).

For each binomial:
- **Coloured solid lines** = model preference (`plogis(pref) − 0.5`) across training, one per model size
- **Dashed gold line** = corpus RelFreq (`freq_prob_c = plogis(log(α/β)) − 0.5`; same for all sizes)
- **Horizontal purple line** = AbsPref (`GenPref_centered`, constant, not training-dependent)

```{r select-case-binoms}
case_binoms <- disagree_125m |>
  # disagree_125m already filtered to abspref_ok_125m & opposite directions
  group_by(corpus) |>
  slice_max(abs(signed_gap_125m), n = N_CASE_BINOMS) |>
  ungroup()

cat(sprintf("Case study binomials: %d BabyLM, %d C4\n",
  sum(case_binoms$corpus == "BabyLM"), sum(case_binoms$corpus == "C4")))
print(case_binoms |>
  transmute(corpus, binom,
            relfreq_prob = freq_prob_c,      # P(alpha) - 0.5
            abspref_prob = GenPref_centered,  # P(alpha) - 0.5
            signed_gap_125m))
```

```{r load-case-traj}
case_binom_vec <- unique(case_binoms$binom)
message(sprintf("Loading trajectories for %d binomials (single DuckDB scan)...", length(case_binom_vec)))

# Single scan across ALL training checkpoints, filtered to case binomials only.
t0 <- proc.time()
freq_traj <- batch_dyn_freq(ck_meta_full, binom_filter = case_binom_vec)
message(sprintf("  DuckDB fetch done in %.1f s (%d rows)", (proc.time() - t0)[["elapsed"]], nrow(freq_traj)))

dat_traj <- train |>
  filter(binom %in% case_binom_vec) |>
  inner_join(ck_meta_full, by = c("model", "step")) |>
  attach_freq(freq_traj) |>
  mutate(
    corpus     = parse_corpus(model),
    model_size = factor(parse_size(model), levels = SIZE_LEVELS),
    pref_prob  = plogis(preference) - 0.5   # P(alpha) - 0.5 scale
  ) |>
  filter(!is.na(tokens), !is.na(log_freq_ratio))

message(sprintf("Trajectory dataset: %d rows", nrow(dat_traj)))
```

### Notable case: "days and years" in C4

In C4, **"years and days"** (β ordering) is more common than **"days and years"** (α ordering):
455 vs. 795 occurrences at end of training. This reflects the natural English tendency
to order temporal units from largest to smallest (years → days). Here are 5 example
sentences from the C4 corpus containing "years and days":

> 1. "The reduction is based on the length of time (in **years and days**) that you retire early."
>
> 2. "...counting the **years and days** till retirement (the 'Reginald Perrin syndrome')."
>
> 3. "Every culture must choose a time in history at which to begin its count of **years and days**."
>
> 4. "Extended term is term life insurance purchased in advance by your cash values for a
>    predetermined number of **years and days**."
>
> 5. "The EAU is a page of **years and days** to read with the best Creating Inclusive School
>    Communities in the l of science." *(garbled OCR)*

The consistent pattern across examples 1–4 is that "years and days" appears as a
**duration span** (coarse-to-fine ordering), which conflicts with the abstract preference
for "days and years" captured in AbsPref.

```{r plot-case-traj, fig.height=5, results='asis'}
for (corp in c("BabyLM", "C4")) {
  these_binoms <- case_binoms |> filter(corpus == corp) |> pull(binom)

  for (binom_i in these_binoms) {
    d <- filter(dat_traj, corpus == corp, binom == binom_i, !is.na(log_freq_ratio))
    if (nrow(d) == 0) {
      cat(sprintf("\n*No data for %s / %s.*\n\n", corp, binom_i))
      next
    }

    abspref_val <- d$GenPref_centered[!is.na(d$GenPref_centered)][1]  # already P(alpha)-0.5

    d_smallmodel <- d |> filter(model_size == first(levels(d$model_size))) |>
      distinct(tokens, freq_prob_c)
    relfreq_final_val <- d_smallmodel |> arrange(desc(tokens)) |> slice(1) |> pull(freq_prob_c)

    # Corpus-level final counts: take the checkpoint with the most tokens in this corpus
    # (alpha_seen/beta_seen are cumulative, so max tokens = fullest count)
    final_counts <- d |>
      filter(tokens == max(tokens, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
      slice(1)
    alpha_n <- formatC(final_counts$alpha_seen, format = "d", big.mark = ",")
    beta_n  <- formatC(final_counts$beta_seen,  format = "d", big.mark = ",")

    p <- ggplot(d, aes(x = tokens, y = pref_prob,
                        colour = model_size, fill = model_size, shape = model_size)) +
      # Zero line
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey60", linewidth = 0.35) +
      # AbsPref reference (constant, P(alpha)-0.5 scale)
      geom_hline(yintercept = abspref_val, colour = "#9e3d8e",
                 linetype = "dotdash", linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.85) +
      annotate("text", x = -Inf, y = abspref_val, hjust = -0.05, vjust = -0.45,
               label = sprintf("AbsPref = %.2f", abspref_val), colour = "#9e3d8e", size = 3.2) +
      # Corpus RelFreq trajectory (same for all sizes — use smallest model's data; P(alpha)-0.5)
      geom_line(data = d_smallmodel,
                aes(x = tokens, y = freq_prob_c), colour = "#cc8800",
                linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.9, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
      annotate("text", x = Inf, y = relfreq_final_val,
               hjust = 1.05, vjust = -0.4, label = "RelFreq", colour = "#cc8800", size = 3.2) +
      # Model preference per size
      geom_line(linewidth = 0.85) +
      geom_point(size = 2.0, stroke = 0.5, colour = "grey25") +
      scale_colour_manual(values = SIZE_COLOURS, name = "Model size") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = SIZE_COLOURS, name = "Model size") +
      scale_shape_manual(values = SIZE_SHAPES, name = "Model size") +
      scale_x_continuous("Training tokens",
                         labels = label_number(scale_cut = cut_short_scale()),
                         expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))) +
      coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, NA)) +
      scale_y_continuous("P(\u03b1) \u2212 0.5  (+ = prefer \u03b1 form)", limits = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
      labs(
        title    = sprintf('%s \u2014 "%s"', corp, binom_i),
        subtitle = sprintf(
          "RelFreq P(\u03b1)\u22120.5 = %.2f (predicts %s); AbsPref P(\u03b1)\u22120.5 = %.2f (predicts %s)",
          mean(d$freq_prob_c, na.rm = TRUE),
          if_else(mean(d$freq_prob_c, na.rm = TRUE) > 0, "\u03b1", "\u03b2"),
          abspref_val,
          if_else(abspref_val > 0, "\u03b1", "\u03b2")),
        caption  = sprintf("Corpus counts at end of training: \u03b1 ordering = %s, \u03b2 ordering = %s",
                           alpha_n, beta_n)
      ) +
      theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
      theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))

    print(p)
    cat("\n\n")
  }
}
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Summary

```{r summary-table}
tibble(
  Analysis = c(
    "BabyLM: AbsPref vs RelFreq conflict items (125M follows AbsPref)",
    "  \u2514 Replicates in 350M/1.3B (AbsPref ok in all 3 sizes)",
    "C4: AbsPref vs RelFreq conflict items (125M follows AbsPref)",
    "  \u2514 Replicates in 350M/1.3B (AbsPref ok in all 3 sizes)",
    "BabyLM: near-flat RelFreq items",
    "C4: near-flat RelFreq items"
  ),
  N = c(
    sum(disagree_125m$corpus == "BabyLM"),
    {
      r <- dat_final |>
        semi_join(filter(disagree_125m, corpus == "BabyLM"), by = c("corpus","binom")) |>
        group_by(binom) |>
        summarise(all_ok = all(abspref_ok))
      sum(r$all_ok)
    },
    sum(disagree_125m$corpus == "C4"),
    {
      r <- dat_final |>
        semi_join(filter(disagree_125m, corpus == "C4"), by = c("corpus","binom")) |>
        group_by(binom) |>
        summarise(all_ok = all(abspref_ok))
      sum(r$all_ok)
    },
    n_distinct(dat_flat$binom[dat_flat$corpus == "BabyLM"]),
    n_distinct(dat_flat$binom[dat_flat$corpus == "C4"])
  )
) |>
  kbl() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped"), full_width = FALSE)
```
